name: Instrument Schedule
description: A laboratory instrument booking calendar displaying reservations across multiple instruments over time, with user attribution, maintenance windows, availability status, and quick-book interactions on free slots.

week.description: Week-grid calendar with instruments on the vertical axis and days/hours on the horizontal axis. Each cell represents a bookable time slot. Renders all bookings as colored blocks with user avatars and sample IDs.

day.description: Detailed single-day view with hours on the vertical axis and instruments as horizontal columns. Bookings render taller with more detail (duration, sample count, user, protocol). Ideal for bench-scheduling huddles.

list.description: Chronological agenda listing upcoming bookings as rows with instrument, user, time window, and sample details. Sorted by start time. Best for mobile viewing and printable schedules.

visual_brief: A lab-scheduling grid rendered as a d-section inside a scrollable container. The grid has InstrumentRows stacked vertically on the left axis and a horizontal TimeAxis along the top showing day/hour labels. The left column shows instrument names in semibold text with their InstrumentStatus indicator beside them: a colored 8px dot (green=online, amber=busy, red=offline, blue=maintenance) and the instrument's short-id in mono-data muted ('Thermo-Q Exactive HF' with 'THRM-01' beneath). Each InstrumentRow has a horizontal rail that spans the week, divided into hourly columns (or 30-min in day preset). TimeBlocks render as colored rectangles positioned over their reserved time range; block color is assigned per-user (stable hash to pastel palette) so multiple bookings by the same researcher share color identity. Each block contains a UserAvatar circle (20px) on the left, user name initial, and sample IDs in mono-data small text centered or truncated. Block height fills the row completely. Hovering a block surfaces a BookingDialog tooltip with full booking details: user, protocol name, sample count, start/end times, and buttons to extend, release, or message the user. MaintenanceSlots render with a distinctive hatched diagonal-stripe pattern and blue background with a wrench icon, labeled 'Maintenance'. Free slots are clickable (cursor:pointer) and hover-highlight in a subtle primary tint inviting new bookings; clicking opens the BookingDialog in creation mode. A vertical NowIndicator line with a small clock-icon pill at top crosses the grid at the current time, updating live. Weekends and after-hours cells show a subtle disabled gray tint. Color-coding by user group (badge-color) can override per-user colors when enabled. A legend anchors to the bottom-right summarizing colors and status indicators.

layout_hints.note: Lab scheduling is shared-resource allocation. Conflicts must be visually obvious. Maintenance windows cannot be double-booked. Always show instrument status alongside schedule.
layout_hints.instrument_row_height: InstrumentRow minimum height 48px, maximum 80px. Row header column fixed at 200px width (sticky left on scroll).
layout_hints.time_block_color: TimeBlock colors assigned by stable-hash of user-id to pastel palette — consistent across sessions. Alternative: override with user-group color when configured.
layout_hints.status_indicator: InstrumentStatus dot MUST be 8px circle with icon companion (sun/moon/wrench/x). Never rely on color alone.
layout_hints.maintenance_pattern: MaintenanceSlot MUST use visually distinct diagonal-stripe hatching + blue tint + wrench icon. Not just solid color — must be unmistakable as non-bookable.
layout_hints.now_indicator: NowIndicator vertical line spans full grid height with z-index above time blocks. Updates position every 60s. Includes clock pill at top with current time label.

slots.time-axis: Top horizontal axis showing day/date labels (week preset) or hour labels (day preset). Sticky on scroll. Uses d-annotation small-caps muted. Weekend columns subtly tinted.
slots.instrument-rows: Repeating slot — one row per instrument. Left column (sticky) shows instrument name, InstrumentStatus dot, and short-id. Right rail contains TimeBlocks for that instrument.
slots.time-blocks: Repeating sub-slot — one per booking. Positioned absolutely within instrument row based on start/end times. Colored by user/group. Contains UserAvatar, name initial, sample IDs.
slots.maintenance-slots: Sub-slot for scheduled instrument maintenance windows. Renders with diagonal-stripe pattern, blue tint, and wrench icon. Non-bookable and visually distinct.
slots.instrument-status: Indicator dot + icon combination beside each instrument name showing current state: online (green), busy (amber), offline (red), maintenance (blue).
slots.now-indicator: Vertical line crossing the grid at the current time. Updates live every 60 seconds. Includes clock pill at top showing current time.
slots.booking-dialog: Modal/popover that opens on TimeBlock hover or free-slot click. Create mode has form for new booking (user, protocol, duration, samples). View mode shows booking details with extend/release/message actions.
slots.legend: Bottom-right corner legend summarizing color coding for users/groups, status indicators, and maintenance pattern.

keyboard: Arrow keys: navigate between grid cells
Enter: open booking dialog on focused cell

responsive.mobile: Switches automatically to list preset. TimeBlocks render as rows with instrument name, time window, user, and samples. BookingDialog opens as full-screen sheet.
responsive.tablet: Week grid at reduced column width. InstrumentStatus collapses to icon-only. Booking detail tooltip becomes tap-to-open rather than hover.

transitions.booking-create: new block fade-in + height grow 300ms ease-out
transitions.now-indicator-move: line transform translate 500ms ease-in-out on minute tick

ambient.now-pulse: now-indicator clock pill subtle pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite
ambient.maintenance-shimmer: hatching pattern subtle diagonal-sweep 4s linear infinite

composition.TimeBlock: Block(d-surface, data-interactive, data-user-color, positioned) > [UserAvatar + UserInitial + SampleIDs]
